content.problemStatement:
After a long and fulfilling career, Chef has decided to retire to a farm out in the countryside.

This farm has several cows and chickens, but Chef doesn't know exactly how many of each there are — he can only see that there are NNN legs in total across all the animals.

Note that each cow has 444 legs and each chicken has 222 legs, and it is guaranteed that NNN is even.

With NNN legs in total, what's the **minimum** possible number of animals that can be present on the farm?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain a single integer NNN - the number of legs.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible number of animals that can be present on the farm.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single even integer N, the total number of legs on the farm.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum possible number of animals.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=2 only a chicken fits → 1 animal. For N=4 a cow fits → 1 animal. For N=6 we need one cow (4 legs) and one chicken (2 legs) → 2 animals.
N=8 can be two cows → 2 animals. N=10 needs two cows (8 legs) plus one chicken (2 legs) → 3 animals.

solution.approach: Each animal contributes either 2 (chicken) or 4 (cow) legs. To minimize the number of animals we want to use as many cows as possible because they have more legs. The optimal strategy is: use floor(N/4) cows; if N is divisible by 4 we are done, otherwise N%4 equals 2 and we need exactly one chicken. Hence the answer is ceil(N/4), which can be computed as N/4 + (N%4 != 0).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        ans = n//4 + (1 if n%4 else 0)
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
